text.title: API Authentication Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every api-authentication artefact must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Auth scheme MUST match the caller audience: B2C session → opaque-session; cross-service → JWT or mTLS; partner integration → OAuth2 client-credentials; key-only call → API-key with rotation.
rule.rationale: Mismatched scheme produces structural risk (e.g. long-lived JWT cannot be revoked instantly), regardless of cryptographic strength.

rule.statement: Access-token lifetime MUST be <= 60 minutes for high-revocation-SLA workloads. Refresh tokens (when used) MUST rotate on every use.

rationale.source: OAuth 2.1 draft + Auth0 / Okta operational guidance

rule.rationale: Long-lived tokens cannot satisfy revocation SLA; reuse-detection on refresh tokens catches token theft.

rule.statement: Scheme spec MUST name a concrete revocation path (token-introspection endpoint OR session-store delete OR API-key rotation procedure). "Logout" alone is not a revocation path for JWT.

rationale.source: OWASP API Security Top 10 — Broken Authentication

rule.rationale: JWTs cannot be revoked by client logout; without a server-side path, compromised tokens stay valid until expiry.

rule.statement: JWT MUST NOT be used as the B2C browser session token without a server-side denylist or short-lived (<=15 min) policy paired with refresh rotation.
rule.rationale: Browser-stored JWTs are exfiltrated by XSS; without server-side controls the session lives until token TTL.

rule.statement: API keys MUST have a documented rotation cadence (<= 90 days) AND a graceful overlap window during rotation. Hard-coded never-rotating keys are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Static keys leak via repos and CI logs; rotation cadence bounds the blast radius.
